comment: Consolidated agent-proposed churn-reason classifications, keyed by allmoxy_customer_id. Sourced from _etl_scripts/churn_research_batches/. Each entry carries the proposed_churn_reason, confidence, 3-5 verbatim evidence_quotes (date + quote + interpretation), supporting_facts, alternative_reasons_considered (with rule-out reasons), and recommended_action. Surfaced on /churn-investigator so the reviewer can accept/edit the proposed reason with one click rather than re-investigating from scratch.

12.name: Canary Closets
12.proposed_churn_reason: FALSE POSITIVE — should be reclassified as active

evidence_quotes.quote: Canary & Allmoxy Sales Training Meeting — December 19, 2025 11:15 AM MST 25 mins — sarah@allmoxy.com madison@allmoxy.com sales@canarycc.com ap@canarycc.com — Customer Account Management and Tagging — The team clarified the structured approach to customer account creation and tagging…
evidence_quotes.interpretation: ACTIVELY in sales training as of Dec 2025. Customer is using Allmoxy and onboarding new internal team members (Melissa). This is not a churn.

evidence_quotes.quote: Title: Canary + Allmoxy Date: Mon Mar 24 2025 12:30:00 GMT-0400 — Participants: dambrogio@canarycc.com, operations@canarycc.com, sarah@allmoxy.com — Problem-solving and Technical Support meeting…
evidence_quotes.interpretation: Quarterly+ ongoing CSM sessions all of 2025 — QuickBooks export, financial training, etc. This is an ENGAGED customer.

evidence_quotes.quote: Canary Allmoxy Financials Date: Mon Feb 10 2025 13:00:00 — Participants: beau@allmoxy.com, sarah@allmoxy.com, beaulewis1@gmail.com, dambrogio@canarycc.com — Darren explains different customer categories: franchises, closet customers, and door dealers
evidence_quotes.interpretation: Beau himself was on the Feb 2025 financials call with Darren D'Ambrogio. The customer is highly engaged and is even a multi-vertical Allmoxy customer (franchises + closet + door dealers).

supporting_facts.current_status_in_queue: at_risk_active_recovery — appears the dashboard flagged this incorrectly given the active engagement

alternative_reasons_considered: Any churn reason: actively meeting and growing — RULED OUT
12.recommended_action: DO NOT classify as churn. Add customer_status_overrides.json entry to mark as 'active' (override the at_risk_active_recovery flag). The Oct 2024 last_payment_date in source data may reflect a billing/subscription gap, but the customer is clearly engaged with multiple training sessions in 2025-2026 — they likely shifted to a different billing arrangement (e.g., services-led).

24.name: Timeless Cabinetry

evidence_quotes.quote: Churn risk 12/31/24 — Last login 10/9/24 — Good amount of orders but stopped in October — Payment failed
evidence_quotes.interpretation: Definitive churn note in CSM-owned format. Payment failed in October 2024, customer stopped logging in concurrently. This is the canonical Payment Failure signature: active usage trailing off coincident with a billing failure.

evidence_quotes.quote: Call 8/14/24 with SaraLyn (accountant) — Helped SaraLyn update Allmoxy card on file
evidence_quotes.interpretation: We DID try to remediate the payment-method issue — accountant updated card 5 months before the failure. The Oct 2024 failure may have been a separate card expiration / freeze.

evidence_quotes.quote: Michael's number is invalid or has been deleted by Verizon
evidence_quotes.interpretation: Primary contact's phone number went dead in May 2024 — early signal of disengagement before the payment failure.

supporting_facts.csm_health_assessment_2024_03: Green — 'this customer is in good/great standing and would advocate for Allmoxy - churn would be shocking'
supporting_facts.decline_signature: Healthy → contact phone dies (May 2024) → payment fails (Oct 2024) → no further usage

alternative_reasons_considered: Unresponsive: phone went dead but CSM did make contact via accountant — RULED OUT (we did engage post-issue)
Out of Business: 5-person shop in residential cabinetry — POSSIBLE but no direct evidence
Customer Stakeholder Misalignment: contact change (Michael → SaraLyn the accountant) without a new champion — PARTIAL EVIDENCE
24.recommended_action: Push 'Payment Failure' as primary. Consider 'Customer Stakeholder Misalignment' as a co-factor — the original champion (Michael) went dark before billing failed.

evidence_quotes.quote: Broc Hill Terry didn't realize that there would be upfront costs...build out fee. I acknowledged the fee and initial investment and move the conversation to the benefits afterward;) He ask me to send an email with our booking link.
evidence_quotes.interpretation: Customer Terry was caught off-guard by build-out fees on top of monthly subscription. Price-shock at the implementation-cost line.

evidence_quotes.quote: Sales call notes Focus on cabinets instead of all the other stuff. 50 colors Biggest interest is in 3D designer Uses 2020 and Pro Kitchen Generic Catalog and build up from there. Interested in a payment plan. 6-12 months implementing over time not right away.
evidence_quotes.interpretation: Customer asked for PAYMENT PLAN and explicitly wanted 6-12 month gradual implementation — price/cash sensitivity.

evidence_quotes.quote: First 2 months $250/month Then $399/ following months
evidence_quotes.interpretation: Stepped pricing arrangement ($250 → $399) to land deal — concessions to overcome price objection.

evidence_quotes.quote: @Samantha Racker Should I re-engage? I can't tell if they were clients that cancelled, or what the situation is with these guys.
evidence_quotes.interpretation: Sep 2024: even Allmoxy team didn't know if Detroit had cancelled — implying low engagement after onboarding.

supporting_facts.decline_signature: Build-out fee shock → stepped pricing concession → slow implementation request → never gained traction

alternative_reasons_considered: Failed Implementation: implementation never completed — STRONG SECONDARY
Timing Not Right: customer asked for 6-12 month ramp — POSSIBLE
51.recommended_action: Push 'Pricing' as primary (build-out fee + monthly subscription was too much for a 5-person shop).

evidence_quotes.quote: Discussed pricing with Larry at $250 for the first 3 months and the remaining 9 at $618 per month. He is supposed to call me monday
evidence_quotes.interpretation: Mar 5 2021: pricing terms confirmed ($250→$618). Mar 18: cancelled and refunded. 13 days from pricing-commit to cancel — likely Larry never even started.

supporting_facts.decline_signature: Pricing agreed Mar 5 → cancelled with refund Mar 18 (13-day onboarding failure)

alternative_reasons_considered: Paid and Never Used: literally never used — STRONG SECONDARY
Pricing: agreed to pricing, didn't churn over price — RULED OUT
53.recommended_action: Push 'Failed Implementation' as primary (13-day flameout, instance deleted, refund issued). Alternatively could be 'Paid and Never Used'. UT-local, Beau may know Larry.
